ingredients.name: Farmer-soldier’s House in Osamunai
ingredients.type: ヘリテージビルディング
categories: ヘリテージビルディング
ingredients.address: Historical Village of Hokkaido, 50-1 Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This tondenhei (farmer-soldier) house at the Historical Village of Hokkaido is faithfully recreated in Golden Kamuy as the style of rural Hokkaido dwelling where Ainu culture and Meiji-era frontier life intersect throughout the series. Fans visit to experience the same rustic interior that frames key early scenes with Asirpa and Sugimoto.

ingredients.name: Kondo Clinic
ingredients.address: Historical Village of Hokkaido, 50-1 Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kondo Clinic building at the Historical Village of Hokkaido served as visual reference for the medical facilities and town buildings seen in Golden Kamuy's Hokkaido frontier settings. Its Western-influenced Meiji architecture captures the collision of old and new Japan that defines the anime's world.

ingredients.name: Aoyama Family Structures for Herring Fishing
ingredients.type: ヘリテージビルディング
categories: ヘリテージビルディング
ingredients.address: Historical Village of Hokkaido, 50-1 Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Golden Kamuy extensively features the Hokkaido herring fishing industry as economic backdrop, and this preserved Aoyama family fishing complex at the Historical Village directly inspired the show's coastal fishing settlement scenes. The authentic Meiji-era herring processing buildings are a must-see for understanding the series' Hokkaido setting.

ingredients.name: Takei Sake Brewery
ingredients.address: Historical Village of Hokkaido, Shimonopporo-50-1, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Takei Sake Brewery at the Historical Village of Hokkaido appears in Golden Kamuy as inspiration for the Meiji-era commercial buildings and taverns that Sugimoto and crew frequent during their gold hunt. The preserved wooden brewery structure evokes the frontier town atmosphere central to the anime.

ingredients.name: Yamamoto Barbar Shop
ingredients.type: ヘリテージビルディング
categories: ヘリテージビルディング
ingredients.address: Historical Village of Hokkaido, 50-1 Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Meiji-era barbershop at the Historical Village of Hokkaido is directly referenced in Golden Kamuy's depiction of frontier-town service establishments. The building's period-accurate interior was used as visual reference for scenes showing early 20th-century Hokkaido daily life.

ingredients.name: Sanmasu Kawamoto Soba Noodle Shop
ingredients.address: 50-1 Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0007, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Sanmasu Kawamoto Soba Shop at the Historical Village of Hokkaido inspired the food stalls and eateries where Sugimoto enthusiastically devours Hokkaido cuisine in Golden Kamuy. The anime's signature food scenes celebrating Hokkaido delicacies draw heavily on this type of Meiji-era establishment.

ingredients.name: Urakawa Subprefactual Government Building
ingredients.address: 50-1, 開拓の村, Atsubetsucho, Atsubetsu Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 004-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The imposing Urakawa Subprefectural Government building at the Historical Village of Hokkaido served as visual reference for the government and military administrative buildings that appear throughout Golden Kamuy, reflecting the Meiji state's expanding authority over Hokkaido. It is one of the most recognizable structures fans seek out.

ingredients.name: Matsuhashi Family Residence
ingredients.entityContext: This preserved Meiji-era family residence at the Historical Village of Hokkaido represents the domestic living spaces depicted in Golden Kamuy, where scenes of ordinary Hokkaido settler life provide contrast to the intense gold-hunt action. The authentic period architecture helps fans visualize the world Sugimoto and Asirpa inhabit.
